1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks

6

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional

6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None
66

6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)
45

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Overall Project Structure

```
bookmark-cli/
├── pyproject.toml              # Project configuration
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py            # Empty package init
│   ├── cli.py                 # CLI entry point and command routing
│   ├── models.py              # Bookmark data model
│   ├── storage.py             # Bookmark persistence layer
│   └── formatter.py           # Output formatting (table/JSON)
└── tests/
    ├── __init__.py
    ├── test_storage.py        # Tests for storage operations
    └── test_search.py         # Tests for search functionality (not yet implemented)
```

**Key Configuration** (`pyproject.toml`):
- Project: `bookmark-cli` version 1.0.0
- Python: >=3.10
- CLI entry point: `bookmark = "src.cli:main"`
- Testing: pytest >= 7.4.0

---

### 2. Main CLI Entry Point and Command Structure

**File**: `/src/cli.py`

The CLI uses `argparse` to build a command-based system with subparsers. Current commands:

- **`add`** - Add a new bookmark
  - Arguments: `url` (required), `title` (required)
  - Options: `--tags` (multiple values), `--notes` (string)
  
- **`list`** - List all bookmarks
  - Options: `--format` (choices: "table", "json", default: "table")
  
- **`delete`** - Delete a bookmark by ID
  - Arguments: `id` (required)
  
- **`show`** - Show a single bookmark
  - Arguments: `id` (required)

The `main()` function dispatches commands to storage and formatting functions.

---

### 3. Data Model for Bookmarks

**File**: `/src/models.py`

The `Bookmark` dataclass contains:

```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str                          # UUID string
    url: str                         # Bookmark URL
    title: str                       # Bookmark title
    tags: List[str] = []             # List of tags (default empty)
    notes: str = ""                  # Optional notes (default empty)
    created_at: str = ISO datetime   # ISO format timestamp (auto-generated)
```

**Storage Format**: JSON array with each bookmark as an object
- Location: `~/.bookmarks.json` (configurable)
- Each bookmark is serialized via `to_dict()` method
- Deserialized via `from_dict()` classmethod

Example JSON structure:
```json
[
  {
    "id": "aaa-111",
    "url": "https://example.com",
    "title": "Example",
    "tags": ["example", "test"],
    "notes": "A sample bookmark",
    "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00"
  }
]
```

---

### 4. All Existing Commands and Implementations

**File**: `/src/storage.py`

**Core Functions**:

1. **`load_bookmarks(db_path)`** - Loads all bookmarks from JSON
   - Returns empty list if file doesn't exist
   - Converts JSON objects to Bookmark instances

2. **`save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)`** - Persists bookmarks to JSON
   - Uses `indent=2` for readability
   - Calls `to_dict()` on each bookmark

3. **`add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path)`** - Creates and saves new bookmark
   - Generates UUID for each bookmark
   - Returns the created Bookmark object
   - Appends to existing bookmarks and saves

4. **`delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** - Deletes a bookmark by ID
   - Returns `True` if found and deleted
   - Returns `False` if ID not found
   - Filters out matching bookmark and re-saves

5. **`get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** - Retrieves single bookmark by ID
   - Returns `Bookmark` object or `None` if not found
   - Linear search through all bookmarks

---

### 5. Output Formatting

**File**: `/src/formatter.py`

**Two formatting functions**:

1. **`format_table(bookmarks)`** - Plain-text table format
   - Column widths: ID (8 chars), Title (30), URL (40), Tags (20)
   - Shows truncated versions of fields with padding
   - Includes header and separator line
   - Returns "No bookmarks found." if list is empty
   - Converts tag list to comma-separated string

   Example output:
   ```
   ID       Title                          URL                                      Tags
   -------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------------- --------------------
   aaa-111  Python Tutorial                https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index  python, tutorial, d
   ```

2. **`format_json(bookmarks)`** - JSON format
   - Returns JSON-serialized list of bookmark dicts
   - Uses 2-space indentation
   - Calls `to_dict()` on each bookmark

---

### 6. Test Structure and Patterns

**File**: `/tests/test_storage.py`

Test structure uses:
- **pytest** framework
- **Fixtures** for setup: `db_path` (temp file), `sample_bookmarks` (predefined test data)
- **Class-based organization**: One test class per function being tested

**Test Classes**:

- **`TestLoadBookmarks`**
  - Tests: missing file returns empty list, loads bookmarks correctly

- **`TestSaveBookmarks`**
  - Tests: creates file, file contains valid JSON

- **`TestAddBookmark`**
  - Tests: adds and returns bookmark, persists to disk, generates unique IDs

- **`TestDeleteBookmark`**
  - Tests: deletes existing bookmark (returns True), returns False for missing ID

- **`TestGetBookmark`**
  - Tests: returns bookmark by ID, returns None for missing ID

**Test Patterns**:
- Uses `tmp_path` pytest fixture for isolated temp files
- Assertions check return values, object properties, file existence
- Tests verify persistence by loading after operations
- Uses `db_path` parameter to avoid touching default `~/.bookmarks.json`

---

### 7. Search Feature Tests (Not Yet Implemented)

**File**: `/tests/test_search.py`

This defines the expected behavior for a search module (`src/search.py`) that doesn't exist yet. The tests import from `src.search`:

```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

**Expected Search Functions**:

1. **`search_by_title(bookmarks, title_query)`** - Case-insensitive substring matching
   - Returns list of matching bookmarks
   - Empty list if no matches

2. **`search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag_query)`** - Exact tag matching
   - Returns list of bookmarks with the tag
   - Empty list if no matches

3. **`search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain_query)`** - URL domain matching
   - Matches both exact domains and subdomains
   - Example: `docs.python.org` matches 2 bookmarks

4. **`search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)`** - Combined search
   - **AND logic**: All provided criteria must match
   - Returns all bookmarks if no criteria provided
   - Returns empty list if criteria conflict
   - Example: `search(title="python", tag="sdk")` finds only bookmarks with both

**Test Fixtures**:

The test file includes a `bookmarks` fixture with 5 sample bookmarks:
- Python Tutorial (tags: python, tutorial, docs)
- RunPod Python SDK (tags: python, sdk, runpod)
- MDN JavaScript Guide (tags: javascript, docs, mdn)
- Flask Web Framework (tags: python, flask, web)
- argparse documentation (tags: python, cli, docs)

**CLI Integration Tests**:

- CLI parser should accept `search` command with flags
- `--title TEXT` - search in title
- `--tag TAG` - search by tag
- `--domain DOMAIN` - search by domain
- `--format [table|json]` - output format support

---

### 8. Utility Functions for Search Feature Reuse

**From `/src/storage.py`**:
- `load_bookmarks()` - Load all bookmarks (needed as data source)
- `save_bookmarks()` - Could be reused if search results need persistence

**From `/src/models.py`**:
- `Bookmark.to_dict()` - Serialize bookmark (useful for output)
- `Bookmark.from_dict()` - Deserialize bookmark (for storage)

**From `/src/formatter.py`**:
- `format_table()` - Render results as table (reusable for search output)
- `format_json()` - Render results as JSON (reusable for search output)

**Tag Query Pattern to Build On**:
- Tags are stored as `List[str]` in Bookmark
- Existing tests show tag matching should be exact and case-sensitive
- Tags are comma-separated in table display

**Domain Extraction Pattern Needed**:
- Tests show need to extract domain from URL (e.g., `github.com`, `docs.python.org`)
- Should match subdomains

---

### Key Insights for Search Implementation

1. **Search Functions Need**: Takes bookmark list + optional filter parameters, returns filtered list

2. **Tag Matching**: Direct list membership check on `bookmark.tags`

3. **Title Matching**: Case-insensitive substring search using `.lower()`

4. **Domain Matching**: Extract domain from URL, check for substring match with URL

5. **AND Logic**: Filter sequentially - if title provided, filter first, then apply tag filter, then domain

6. **CLI Integration**: Add `search` subparser to argparse in `cli.py` with optional flags for title/tag/domain/format

7. **Output Reuse**: Both search and list commands can use the same `format_table()` and `format_json()` functions

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. `tests/test_search.py` already exists and defines the full expected behaviour — it just has no implementation to run against. The goal is to make all those tests pass by creating `src/search.py` and wiring a `search` subcommand into the CLI.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **New file** — all search logic |
| `src/cli.py` | Add `search` subparser + dispatch handler |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Defines all expected behaviour (do not modify) |
| `src/formatter.py` | `format_table()` / `format_json()` — reuse as-is |
| `src/storage.py` | `load_bookmarks()` — reuse as-is |
| `src/models.py` | `Bookmark` dataclass — fields: `id`, `url`, `title`, `tags`, `notes`, `created_at` |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Create a new file with four functions. **No new dependencies** — stdlib `urllib.parse` only.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark

def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = title_query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]

def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against a bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag_query in b.tags]

def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL hostname equals or is a subdomain of domain_query."""
    def matches(url: str) -> bool:
        hostname = urlparse(url).hostname or ""
        return hostname == domain_query or hostname.endswith("." + domain_query)
    return [b for b in bookmarks if matches(b.url)]

def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combined AND search: each provided criterion must match."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Domain logic detail:** `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/...").hostname` → `"docs.python.org"`. A query of `"docs.python.org"` hits the `==` branch; a query of `"python.org"` hits the `.endswith(".python.org")` branch. Both test cases (`github.com` exact and `docs.python.org` subdomain) pass with this logic.

---

### Step 2 — Add `search` subparser in `src/cli.py`

In `build_parser()`, add after the `show` block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring (case-insensitive)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag",   default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain",default=None, help="Filter by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

---

### Step 3 — Add dispatch handler in `main()` in `src/cli.py`

Add a new `elif` branch (after `show`) using already-imported `load_bookmarks`, `format_table`, `format_json`, plus the new `search` function:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    from .search import search
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## What Is Reused (No Changes Needed)

- `format_table(bookmarks)` — already handles empty list ("No bookmarks found.") and list of `Bookmark` objects
- `format_json(bookmarks)` — same interface
- `load_bookmarks()` — loads from default `~/.bookmarks.json`
- `Bookmark` dataclass — no changes

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:

```bash
cd /Users/timpietrusky/bookmark-cli
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `tests/test_search.py` and `tests/test_storage.py` pass.

Spot-check the CLI parser tests specifically:
- `test_search_command_exists` — verifies `build_parser()` accepts `search --title python`
- `test_search_format_json_flag` — verifies `--tag docs --format json` parses correctly

Manual smoke test (optional):
```bash
bookmark add https://docs.python.org/3/ "Python Docs" --tags python docs
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --domain docs.python.org
```

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. `tests/test_search.py` already exists and defines the full expected behaviour — it just has no implementation to run against. The goal is to make all those tests pass by creating `src/search.py` and wiring a `search` subcommand into the CLI.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **New file** — all search logic |
| `src/cli.py` | Add `search` subparser + dispatch handler |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Defines all expected behaviour (do not modify) |
| `src/formatter.py` | `format_table()` / `format_json()` — reuse as-is |
| `src/storage.py` | `load_bookmarks()` — reuse as-is |
| `src/models.py` | `Bookmark` dataclass — fields: `id`, `url`, `title`, `tags`, `notes`, `created_at` |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Create a new file with four functions. **No new dependencies** — stdlib `urllib.parse` only.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark

def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = title_query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]

def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against a bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag_query in b.tags]

def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain_query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL hostname equals or is a subdomain of domain_query."""
    def matches(url: str) -> bool:
        hostname = urlparse(url).hostname or ""
        return hostname == domain_query or hostname.endswith("." + domain_query)
    return [b for b in bookmarks if matches(b.url)]

def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combined AND search: each provided criterion must match."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Domain logic detail:** `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/...").hostname` → `"docs.python.org"`. A query of `"docs.python.org"` hits the `==` branch; a query of `"python.org"` hits the `.endswith(".python.org")` branch. Both test cases (`github.com` exact and `docs.python.org` subdomain) pass with this logic.

---

### Step 2 — Add `search` subparser in `src/cli.py`

In `build_parser()`, add after the `show` block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring (case-insensitive)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag",   default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain",default=None, help="Filter by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

---

### Step 3 — Add dispatch handler in `main()` in `src/cli.py`

Add a new `elif` branch (after `show`) using already-imported `load_bookmarks`, `format_table`, `format_json`, plus the new `search` function:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    from .search import search
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## What Is Reused (No Changes Needed)

- `format_table(bookmarks)` — already handles empty list ("No bookmarks found.") and list of `Bookmark` objects
- `format_json(bookmarks)` — same interface
- `load_bookmarks()` — loads from default `~/.bookmarks.json`
- `Bookmark` dataclass — no changes

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:

```bash

python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `tests/test_search.py` and `tests/test_storage.py` pass.

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_single_criterion_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

    def test_single_criterion_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.title == "python"

    def test_search_format_json_flag(self):
        """The CLI should accept --format json on the search command."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
